title: katoella beddomei
intro: Katoella beddomei is a plant species, also known as the Mysore broom tree, native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Katoella beddomei include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent cancer. Herbal preparations of Katoella beddomei include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are often used to make medicinal teas, creams, and capsules.
